payload.key_insight: Broadcom's explosive AI semiconductor revenue growth (106% YoY, $8.4B in Q1 accelerating to $10.7B in Q2) driven by 'robust demand for custom AI accelerators' is strongly relevant to Apple, which is a known Broadcom customer for custom silicon and networking components; sustained AI chip demand at Broadcom implies healthy procurement pipelines and validates Apple's own AI-driven silicon investment cycle. However, the signal is partially muted because Broadcom's AI growth is concentrated on hyperscaler/cloud customers rather than consumer device makers, limiting direct read-through to AAPL near-term revenue or EPS estimates.
specific_quotes: Q1 AI revenue of $8.4 billion grew 106% year-over-year, above our forecast, driven by robust demand for custom AI accelerators and AI networking.
Our AI revenue growth is accelerating, and we expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2.
In Q2 we expect revenue growth to increase 47% year-over-year to $22.0 billion, with adjusted EBITDA of 68%.
